content:
How to properly reboot or reset your Oassis Router:
1. Simple Reboot (Fixes 80% of slow speeds): Unplug the power cable from the back of the router, wait 30 seconds, and plug it back in. Wait 3 minutes for the lights to stabilize.
2. Full Factory Reset (ONLY if reboot fails or you forgot your password): Locate the red reset button inside the pinhole on the back. Use a paperclip to press and hold it for 10 seconds. Release when the 'SYS' light turns red. Wait 3 minutes. Note: This will erase your custom Wi-Fi name and password.

Router Light Status Guide:
- PON (Solid Green): Good. Fiber connection is established.
- PON (Blinking Green): Trying to connect to the ISP. Wait 2-3 minutes.
- LOS (Red): CRITICAL. Fiber cable is disconnected, broken, or bent. Check the thin green/white cable. If it is visibly damaged, a technician must replace it.
- SYS (Green): System operating normally.
- LOS + PON (Both Off): The router is not receiving power or is completely dead.

topic: No Internet on One Device vs All Devices
content:
Troubleshooting Isolation Steps:
1. If ONLY your phone has no internet: Go to phone Wi-Fi settings, 'Forget' the network, and reconnect by entering the password manually.
2. If ONLY your PC has no internet: Check if it is connected via Wi-Fi or Ethernet cable. Try disabling and re-enabling the network adapter.
3. If ALL devices have no internet: The issue is with the router or the ISP line. Proceed to rebooting the router. If the LOS light is red, check the fiber cable.

How to find or change your Wi-Fi password:
1. Check the sticker on the back or bottom of the router. The default Wi-Fi name (SSID) and password (WPA key) are printed there.
2. If you changed it and forgot it, you must do a Factory Reset (press the reset pinhole for 10 seconds). This will reset the password back to the one on the sticker.
3. Once logged in, we strongly recommend changing the password via the router admin page at 192.168.1.1.

PPPoE Authentication Error (Usually means account suspension or cable issue):
1. This error means the router cannot verify your account with our servers.
2. First, check if your LOS light is red. If yes, it's a physical cable issue, not an account issue.
3. If lights are green, this usually means your account is suspended due to an unpaid bill. Check your balance.
4. Restart the router. If the error persists for more than 15 minutes, contact support.

To configure DNS on Windows (Fixes 'Connected but no internet' issues):
1. Open Control Panel > Network and Internet > Network and Sharing Center.
2. Click 'Change adapter settings' on the left.
3. Right-click your active connection -> 'Properties'.
4. Select 'Internet Protocol Version 4 (TCP/IPv4)' -> 'Properties'.
5. Choose 'Use the following DNS server addresses': Set Primary to 8.8.8.8 and Secondary to 1.1.1.1. Click OK.

Wi-Fi Optimization Tips:
1. Placement: Keep the router in an open, elevated area. Never put it on the floor, behind a TV, or inside a closed cabinet.
2. Interference: Keep the router away from microwaves, Bluetooth devices, and thick concrete walls.
3. 5GHz vs 2.4GHz: Use 5GHz for high speed in the same room. Use 2.4GHz if you are far away or behind walls, as it penetrates obstacles better but is slower.

Fiber Optic Cable Care:
The fiber cable is extremely fragile. Never bend it at a sharp angle (under 30mm radius), never staple it to the wall, and never step on it. If the cable is bent or crushed, the internet will drop entirely and the LOS light will turn red. If the cable is visibly broken or cut, do not try to tape it together—it requires a special splice. A technician must be dispatched to replace it.

How to accurately test your internet speed:
1. Do NOT test over Wi-Fi if you suspect low speeds. Connect your laptop directly to the router using an Ethernet cable.
2. Close all background downloads, torrents, and streaming apps (Netflix, YouTube) on ALL devices in the house.
3. Go to speedtest.net or speedtest.tn and run the test.
4. If the result is above 85% of your plan speed (e.g., 85 Mbps on a 100 Mbps plan), your line is healthy. If it is much lower, contact support.

We offer several convenient ways to pay your internet bill:
1. Bank Transfer: Transfer the exact amount to our IBAN (TN1234567890123456789012) using your account number as the reference.
2. Flouci: Send the payment via the Flouci app to our official merchant number.
3. Post Office: Pay in cash at any Tunisian post office counter by providing your account number (e.g., 4821).
4. E-Dinar: Use your e-Dinar card at any approved electronic payment point.

Our current available internet plans are:
1. Fiber 100: 100 Mbps download/upload - 29.9 TND/month.
2. Fiber 500: 500 Mbps download/upload - 49.9 TND/month.
3. Fiber 1Giga: 1 Gbps download/upload - 79.9 TND/month.
All plans include free router rental, no installation fees for new subscribers, and unlimited data.

Late Payment Policy:
1. A grace period of 5 days is allowed after the due date without penalty.
2. After 5 days, a late fee of 2 TND will be applied to your next bill.
3. If the bill remains unpaid for 15 days past the due date, your internet service will be temporarily suspended until the balance is cleared.
4. To reactivate, simply pay the overdue balance plus any applicable late fees. Service usually resumes automatically within 2 hours of payment confirmation.

To upgrade or change your internet plan:
1. You can request a plan change by asking me to create a support ticket for 'Plan Upgrade'.
2. Upgrades take effect within 24 hours. Downgrades take effect at the start of your next billing cycle.
3. The price difference will be prorated on your next invoice if you upgrade mid-cycle.
4. There are no hidden fees for changing your plan.

If you believe there is an error on your bill (e.g., double charge, wrong amount):
1. Do not pay the disputed amount yet if you suspect an error.
2. Ask me to create a billing support ticket. Provide the amount you were charged and why you think it is wrong.
3. Our billing department will review your account usage logs and issue a correction or refund within 3-5 business days if an error is confirmed.
